Title: A Glycan Array-Based Assay for the Identification and Characterization of Plant Glycosyltransferases

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: In vitro glycan array assay using recombinant plant enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: β-1,4-galactan side chains of rhamnogalacturonan I (RG-I)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to plant cell wall architecture and biomass properties
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: UDP-galactose; clickable UDP-6-N3-Gal and UDP-4-N3-Gal; weakly UDP-4-N3-Xyl
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Elongated β-1,4-galactan; low-level unnatural xylosylated galactan products
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from UDP-Gal to growing β-1,4-galactan side chains in RG-I; also weakly transfers azido-xylose from UDP-Xyl derivative to galactan acceptors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes addition of galactose to β-1,4-galactan acceptors on the array; shows acceptor substitution selectivity and donor promiscuity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGALS1 transferred clickable UDP-Gal donors to β-1,4-linked galactan oligosaccharides on the glycan array. 2. The enzyme tolerated 3-position arabinofuranosyl substitutions on acceptors but not 6-position β-galactosyl or arabinofuranosyl substitutions. 3. UDP-6-N3-Gal was transferred more extensively than UDP-4-N3-Gal, consistent with less chain termination. 4. AtGALS1 also weakly accepted clickable UDP-Xyl, revealing donor promiscuity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in HEK293 cells; glycan array transfer assay; click-chemistry detection on chip
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro glycan array assay with synthetic acceptors and functionalized sugar donors
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a benchmark well-characterized plant GT in the assay platform; all enzymes were expressed as soluble truncated proteins in HEK293 cells.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering RG-I galactan structure and generating unnatural galactan/xylogalactan materials; relevant to modifying biomass properties.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: In vitro glycan array assay using recombinant plant enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: β-1,4-galactan side chains of rhamnogalacturonan I (RG-I)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to plant cell wall architecture and biomass properties
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from clickable UDP-Gal donors to galactan oligosaccharide acceptors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transfers clickable UDP-Gal donors with acceptor-recognition pattern very similar to AtGALS1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtGALS1 accepted both clickable UDP-Gal donors on the glycan array. 2. Its acceptor specificity was very similar to that of AtGALS1, with only slight differences.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in HEK293 cells; glycan array transfer assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Analyzed as a related enzyme to AtGALS1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for altering RG-I galactan structure in woody biomass engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: In vitro glycan array assay using recombinant plant enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: β-1,4-galactan side chains of rhamnogalacturonan I (RG-I)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to plant cell wall architecture and biomass properties
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose from clickable UDP-Gal donors to galactan oligosaccharide acceptors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transfers clickable UDP-Gal donors with acceptor-recognition pattern closely resembling AtGALS1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGALS2, a close AtGALS1 ortholog, transferred both clickable UDP-Gal donors. 2. Only slight differences in recognized acceptors were observed relative to AtGALS1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in HEK293 cells; glycan array transfer assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Characterized as a close ortholog of AtGALS1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool to alter pectic galactan architecture.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: In vitro glycan array assay using recombinant plant enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to AGP glycan assembly and cell wall-associated functions
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactose to substituted and unsubstituted β-1,3-galactan acceptors, indicating β-1,3-galactosylation activity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts on β-1,3-galactan rather than purely β-1,6-galactan acceptors, refining its functional assignment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGalT31A galactosylated substituted and unsubstituted β-1,3-galactan oligosaccharides on the array. 2. The data indicate β-1,3-galactosylation rather than the previously implied β-1,6-galactosylation activity. 3. Acceptor substitution pattern influenced transfer efficiency.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in HEK293 cells; glycan array transfer assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously implicated in β-1,6-linked galactan synthesis in AGPs, but this study suggests β-1,3-galactosylation activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering AGP glycan fine structure.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: In vitro glycan array assay using recombinant plant enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to AGP glycan maturation and cell wall properties
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Fucosylated oligosaccharides containing arabinofuranose α-1,3-linked to galactose
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers fucose to AGP-like oligosaccharides containing arabinofuranose α-1,3-linked to galactose
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Fucosylates essentially all tested acceptors containing α-1,3-linked arabinofuranose on galactose
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtFUT4 used clickable GDP-Fuc donor to fucosylate AGP-related acceptors on the array. 2. It recognized essentially all oligosaccharides containing arabinofuranose residues α-1,3-linked to galactose.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in HEK293 cells; glycan array transfer assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its AGP arabinose fucosylation activity was consistent with previous reports.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to modulate AGP fucosylation patterns.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to AGP glycan maturation and cell wall properties
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Fucosylated oligosaccharides containing arabinofuranose α-1,3-linked to galactose
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers fucose to AGP-like oligosaccharides containing arabinofuranose α-1,3-linked to galactose
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Fucosylates essentially all tested acceptors containing α-1,3-linked arabinofuranose on galactose
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtFUT6 used clickable GDP-Fuc donor to fucosylate AGP-related acceptors on the array. 2. It recognized essentially all oligosaccharides containing arabinofuranose residues α-1,3-linked to galactose.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in HEK293 cells; glycan array transfer assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its AGP arabinose fucosylation activity was consistent with previous reports.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to modulate AGP fucosylation patterns.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: In vitro glycan array assay using recombinant plant enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to AGP glycan maturation and cell wall properties
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Fucosylated oligosaccharides containing arabinofuranose α-1,3-linked to galactose
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers fucose to AGP-like oligosaccharides containing arabinofuranose α-1,3-linked to galactose
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Displays acceptor specificity similar to AtFUT4/AtFUT6, supporting its role in AGP biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtFUT7 fucosylated AGP-like acceptors containing α-1,3-linked arabinofuranose on galactose. 2. Its biochemical function had been previously unknown. 3. The shared acceptor specificity with AtFUT4 and AtFUT6 indicates AtFUT7 is likely a previously undiscovered member of the AGP biosynthesis pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in HEK293 cells; glycan array transfer assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: New functional evidence assigned AtFUT7 to AGP biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for engineering AGP fucosylation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: In vitro glycan array assay using recombinant plant enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Xyloglucan side-chain decorated glucans; unnatural galacto-mixed-linkage glucan products in assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to cell wall architecture and biomass properties
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Naturally UDP-xylose; in this assay accepted UDP-6-N3-Gal but not UDP-4-N3-Xyl
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Galactosylated unsubstituted and xylosylated glucan oligosaccharides; no detectable product with clickable UDP-Xyl donor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Normally adds xylose to β-1,4-glucan backbone in xyloglucan; in this assay transferred azido-galactose from UDP-6-N3-Gal to glucan acceptors
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Did not accept clickable UDP-Xyl donor but unexpectedly transferred clickable UDP-Gal to glucan and mixed-linkage glucan acceptors, revealing donor promiscuity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtXXT1 showed no detectable activity with UDP-4-N3-Xyl on the array. 2. It unexpectedly transferred UDP-6-N3-Gal to unsubstituted and xylose-substituted glucan oligosaccharides. 3. AtXXT1 preferred unsubstituted and lowly substituted glucans over highly substituted acceptors. 4. It also recognized mixed-linkage glucans when sufficient β-1,4-glucan stretches were present.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in HEK293 cells; glycan array transfer assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Demonstrates substantial donor/acceptor promiscuity under assay conditions and enables generation of unnatural xylogalactan-type structures.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for glycoengineering novel glucan materials and probing xyloglucan biosynthetic specificity.
